tldr.title: S&P 500 rejects SpaceX, also blocking entry for OpenAI and Anthropic
tldr.author: maltalex
bullets: S&P 500 eligibility rules block private AI leaders OpenAI and Anthropic, limiting their access to massive passive public index capital.
Retail and institutional index investors must rely on proxies like Microsoft and Amazon for exposure to leading frontier AI model developers.
Strict profitability requirements and multi-class share structures prevent venture-backed AI startups from entering major public stock indexes.

tldr.title: ntsc-rs - an accurate VHS video effect
tldr.author: Yogthos
bullets: Rust-based library implementing digital signal processing (DSP) to simulate analog NTSC video signals and VHS tape artifacts with high accuracy.
Supports integration across multiple platforms via a standalone GUI/CLI, WebAssembly (Wasm) builds, and OpenFX or After Effects plugins.
Simulates real composite signal modulation, chroma-luma separation, VHS tracking errors, head switching noise, and tape-wear artifacts at a pixel level.

tldr.title: The Smart TV in Your LivingRoom Is a Node in the AIScraping Economy
tldr.author: gerikson
bullets: Smart TV apps embed residential proxy SDKs, turning home IoT devices into exit nodes for distributed AI web scraping campaigns.
This residential IP rotation bypasses traditional WAFs, rate limiting, and bot detection systems like Cloudflare or Akamai.
Security teams must look beyond IP reputation, implementing behavioral analysis and TLS fingerprinting to block scrapers routing through IoT nodes.

tldr.title: Cloudflare CEO is lying to you about the bot traffic jump
tldr.author: speckx
bullets: Cloudflare's reported bot traffic surges may be inflated by misclassifying legitimate API requests, health checks, and benign monitoring probes.
DevOps teams should verify traffic metrics independently using origin server logs instead of relying solely on Cloudflare dashboard telemetry.
Inflated bot metrics can trigger aggressive WAF blocking rules, causing false positives and disrupting legitimate user and B2B API integrations.

tldr.title: Scientists ejected from diabetes conference for distributing journal reprints
tldr.author: Jennifer Ouellette
bullets: The ADA ejected its journal editor-in-chief Steven Kahn and former president Desmond Schatz from its annual conference.
The ousters were triggered by the unauthorized distribution of physical journal reprints, demonstrating zero-tolerance policies for venue rules.
This high-profile clash highlights ongoing friction between open academic dissemination and strict intellectual property enforcement by scientific publishers.
